concepts.dilemma: 小雅做平面設計，朋友說「你要經營個人品牌」。她開始每天發文、做Reels、回留言，結果真正接案的時間被切碎，還一直覺得自己不夠「成功」。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoya works as a graphic designer. Her friends say, 'You need to build your personal brand.' She starts posting daily, making Reels, and replying to comments. As a result, her actual client work time gets fragmented, and she constantly feels like she's not 'successful' enough.
concepts.solution: 她把「品牌」改成「工作系統」：固定每週只發一次長文、其餘時間只做作品與休息。前兩個月流量掉很兇，她很焦慮，但半年後靠穩定案例與轉介紹，反而接到更適合的案子。
concepts.solution_en: She reframes 'branding' as 'work system': posting only one long-form piece weekly, spending the rest of her time on actual work and rest. Traffic drops significantly in the first two months, causing anxiety. But after six months, steady cases and referrals bring her better-suited clients.

concepts.dilemma: 阿哲被裁員，長輩說「你要反省是不是不夠努力」。他開始把所有失業的痛苦都吞進去，連求助都覺得丟臉。
concepts.dilemma_en: Azhe gets laid off. His elders say, 'You should reflect on whether you worked hard enough.' He starts internalizing all the pain of unemployment, feeling too ashamed even to ask for help.
concepts.solution: 他加入求職互助群，把問題拆成「產業裁撤」「內推門檻」「履歷規格」三件事。剛開始他還是會自責，但一邊互改履歷、一邊共享職缺，最後靠群友內推回到職場，心裡也更不把一切怪自己。
concepts.solution_en: He joins a job-seeking mutual aid group and breaks down the problem into three parts: 'industry downsizing,' 'referral barriers,' and 'resume standards.' He still blames himself at first, but through collaboratively editing resumes and sharing job openings, he eventually returns to work via a referral, learning not to blame everything on himself.

concepts.dilemma: 小榮看到別人年紀輕輕得獎、上雜誌，就覺得「一定是我不夠好」。她越做越保守，只敢做「會被喜歡」的東西。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaorong sees others winning awards and getting featured in magazines at a young age, thinking 'I must not be good enough.' She becomes increasingly conservative, only daring to create things that 'will be liked.'
concepts.solution: 她開始記錄「被看見的路徑」：誰介紹誰、誰在哪裡曝光、誰有資源。她很難接受這不是純實力，但一旦看清規則，就改為經營同儕合作與公共投稿，慢慢累積可見度而不是自我否定。
concepts.solution_en: She begins documenting 'paths to visibility': who introduces whom, who gets exposure where, who has resources. It's hard to accept this isn't pure merit, but once she sees the rules clearly, she shifts to building peer collaborations and public submissions, gradually accumulating visibility instead of self-negation.

concepts.dilemma: 小豪做插畫，客戶說「我們很喜歡你的風格，但預算不多」。他想著「做喜歡的事不要計較」，接了三次後發現自己連房租都快付不出來。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaohao does illustration. Clients say, 'We love your style, but our budget is limited.' Thinking 'Don't be calculative when doing what you love,' he accepts three such projects, only to find he can barely afford rent.
concepts.solution: 他做了簡單的「費率表」與「低預算方案」：少張數、少修改、交付更清楚。第一次講價他手心冒汗，也被拒絕兩次，但第三次他遇到願意尊重規則的客戶，工作變少卻更穩。
concepts.solution_en: He creates a simple 'rate card' and 'low-budget option': fewer pieces, fewer revisions, clearer deliverables. His palms sweat during the first negotiation, and he gets rejected twice, but on the third try he meets a client who respects the rules. Work decreases but becomes more stable.

concepts.dilemma: 工作群裡有人一直說「現在行情就這樣，便宜才有案子」。小芸只好壓價搶案，結果一做就爆肝，還被要求無限修改。
concepts.dilemma_en: In work groups, people keep saying 'The market rate is just like this now, you need to be cheap to get projects.' Xiaoyun has to lower her prices to compete. As a result, every project leads to burnout and endless revision requests.
concepts.solution: 她跟三個同行約定「不接低於某價格的案子」，並互相轉介適合的客戶。前期空窗讓她很恐慌，但半年後客戶開始把她視為「有標準的人」，她也不再靠拼命維生。
concepts.solution_en: She makes a pact with three peers to 'not accept projects below a certain rate,' and they refer suitable clients to each other. The initial gap in work causes panic, but after six months clients start seeing her as 'someone with standards,' and she no longer survives by overworking.

concepts.dilemma: 阿凱接了網站案，對方一開始只要五頁，後來又加活動頁、會員頁、還要改Logo。阿凱覺得「都差不多啦」，做到後來崩潰，卻不敢加價。
concepts.dilemma_en: Amin takes on a website project. The client initially wants five pages, but later adds event pages, member pages, and wants the logo redesigned. Amin thinks 'It's all pretty similar,' but ends up overwhelmed and doesn't dare ask for more money.
concepts.solution: 他學會用「變更單」：每新增一項就寫一段話＋一個新價格。客戶一開始覺得麻煩，但當阿明把交付時間與責任講清楚，對方反而更安心，最後案子雖然慢，但沒有失控。
concepts.solution_en: He learns to use 'change orders': every new item gets documented with a description plus a new price. The client finds it tedious at first, but when Amin clarifies delivery times and responsibilities, they actually feel more secure. The project takes longer but doesn't spiral out of control.

concepts.dilemma: 小真去應徵設計，對方說「先做三張給我們看看」。她熬夜做完，對方一句「不合」就消失，後來她在別人的廣告上看到自己的構圖。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaozhen applies for a design position. They say, 'Create three pieces for us to review first.' She stays up all night to finish, they say 'Not a fit' and disappear. Later, she sees her compositions in someone else's ads.
concepts.solution: 她改成只做「付費試作」或「看過往作品＋短談」的流程。她因此少掉很多機會，也很不甘心，但留下來的合作都更乾淨，後來甚至有公司主動問她的試作費率。
concepts.solution_en: She switches to only doing 'paid test work' or 'review past work + short consultation.' She loses many opportunities and feels reluctant, but the collaborations that remain are cleaner. Later, companies even proactively ask about her test work rates.

concepts.dilemma: 阿真接案被要求「你自己買字體、自己付圖庫、自己買軟體」。他以為這是常態，結果每個月工具費吃掉一大半收入。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaokai takes on projects where he's told, 'You buy the fonts, you pay for stock images, you buy the software.' He thinks this is normal, until monthly tool costs eat up most of his income.
concepts.solution: 他在報價單加一行「工具與授權成本」，並提供替代選項（免費字體/客戶自購）。第一次他被說「你怎麼這麼計較」，但他撐住，後來反而有客戶感謝他把風險講清楚。
concepts.solution_en: He adds a line to his quote: 'Tool and licensing costs,' offering alternatives (free fonts/client purchases). The first time, he's told 'Why are you so calculative,' but he holds firm. Later, clients actually thank him for clarifying the risks.

concepts.dilemma: 小月做自由接案，客戶總說「下個月一起結」。她每次都不好意思催款，直到信用卡循環越滾越大，工作越接越焦慮。
concepts.dilemma_en: Alin freelances, and clients always say 'Let's settle everything next month.' She feels too embarrassed to chase payment each time, until her credit card debt spirals larger, and taking on work becomes increasingly anxiety-inducing.
concepts.solution: 她改為「50%訂金＋分段付款＋逾期停工」。前兩次她催款時心跳很快，也失去一個客戶，但剩下的客戶付款更準時，她的焦慮明顯下降。
concepts.solution_en: She switches to '50% deposit + staged payments + late payment work stoppage.' Her heart pounds during the first two payment chases, and she loses one client, but remaining clients pay more punctually, and her anxiety noticeably decreases.

concepts.dilemma: 小宇把插畫賣給品牌，對方說「買斷比較省事」。他答應了，後來看到對方把圖用在各種商品上，賺很多，但他只能拿一次性費用。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoyu sells his illustrations to a brand. They say, 'A buyout is simpler.' He agrees, then later sees them using his art on all kinds of products, making lots of money, while he only got a one-time fee.
concepts.solution: 他學會用「授權」：限定用途、期間、地區、加價延展。談判時客戶會嫌麻煩，他也會怕失去合作，但當他拿出清楚條款與價格表，反而讓對方知道怎麼買、也比較願意長期合作。
concepts.solution_en: He learns to use 'licensing': defining usage, duration, region, with paid extensions. During negotiations, clients find it tedious and he fears losing collaborations, but when he presents clear terms and a price list, it actually shows them how to buy properly and encourages longer-term cooperation.

concepts.dilemma: 阿德每次案子做完就得立刻找下一單，生活像永遠在追公車。每當空窗，他就懷疑自己是不是快不行了。
concepts.dilemma_en: Ade finishes one project and immediately has to find the next. Life feels like forever chasing buses. Whenever there's a gap, he worries he's about to fail.
concepts.solution: 他開始推「維護合約」：每月固定時數、固定費用。前三個月沒人買，他差點放棄，但第四個老客戶說「這樣我也安心」，從此阿德的收入有了底。
concepts.solution_en: He starts offering 'maintenance contracts': fixed hours per month, fixed fees. For three months no one buys, and he nearly gives up. But the fourth month, an old client says 'This gives me peace of mind too.' From then on, Ade has a baseline income.

concepts.dilemma: 客戶半夜兩點傳訊息「可以快回嗎」，小倩怕失禮就秒回。久了她的睡眠碎掉，白天腦袋像棉花。
concepts.solution: 她建立「回覆時段」與「緊急費率」。剛開始她很怕被討厭，但當她把規則寫在合作文件裡，反而讓客戶知道界線；後來真的急件時，客戶也願意付加急費。
concepts.dilemma_en: A client messages at 2 AM: 'Can you reply quickly?' Xiaoqian fears being rude and replies instantly. Over time, her sleep fragments and her daytime brain feels like cotton.
concepts.solution_en: She establishes 'response hours' and 'emergency rates.' At first she fears being disliked, but when she writes the rules into collaboration documents, it actually clarifies boundaries. Later, when there's truly urgent work, clients are willing to pay the rush fee.

concepts.dilemma: 阿寶做品牌提案，客戶每次都用情緒壓他：「你是不是不在乎我們？」他花很多力氣安撫，卻覺得自己的專業被掏空。
concepts.solution: 他把討論改成「以文件決策」：每次會後寄紀要、請客戶確認。客戶一開始覺得冷，但情緒勒索變少了；他也學會在必要時說「我需要你用具體修改點回覆」。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaozhi works on brand proposals. The client always pressures him emotionally: 'Don't you care about us?' He spends so much energy soothing them, feeling his expertise is being drained.
concepts.solution_en: He shifts discussions to 'document-based decisions': sending meeting notes afterward and asking clients to confirm. Clients initially find it cold, but emotional manipulation decreases. He also learns to say when necessary, 'I need you to respond with specific revision points.'

concepts.dilemma: 小慧覺得不去酒會就沒機會，但她其實怕吵也不喝酒。她硬撐去幾次，回家只剩空虛，還覺得自己很不會做人。
concepts.solution: 她改成「白天咖啡小聚＋一對一介紹」：每週只約一次，且有明確目的。開始時她擔心太慢，但半年後她的人脈變得更深，合作更穩、也更像她自己。
concepts.dilemma_en: Afen feels she won't get opportunities without attending cocktail parties, but she actually dislikes noise and doesn't drink. She forces herself to go a few times, returns home feeling empty, and thinks she's bad at socializing.
concepts.solution_en: She switches to 'daytime coffee meetings + one-on-one introductions': only once per week, with clear purposes. She initially worries it's too slow, but after six months her network becomes deeper, collaborations more stable, and more authentic to herself.

concepts.dilemma: 小彤總覺得「我一定要很獨特」，看到別人做類似風格就心慌。她花很多時間改風格，最後作品集反而散掉。
concepts.solution: 她把焦點改成「議題與方法」：固定關心的主題、固定的研究方式。她一開始怕不夠酷，但當她的案例越來越一致，客戶反而更快理解她的價值。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaotong always feels 'I must be very unique.' When she sees others doing similar styles, she panics. She spends lots of time changing her style, and her portfolio becomes scattered.
concepts.solution_en: She shifts focus to 'topics and methods': consistent themes of interest, consistent research approaches. She initially fears it's not cool enough, but as her cases become more consistent, clients actually understand her value faster.

concepts.dilemma: 阿傑每天列滿待辦，覺得不忙就有罪惡感。久了他做事速度變慢，還開始討厭自己最喜歡的創作。
concepts.solution: 他把行程改成「每週只抓三件大事」，留空白給休息。剛開始他覺得自己在偷懶，但一段時間後品質變好、失誤變少，他也找回對作品的耐心。
concepts.dilemma_en: Ajie fills his to-do list every day, feeling guilty when not busy. Over time, his work speed slows, and he starts hating his favorite creative work.
concepts.solution_en: He changes his schedule to 'only three big things per week,' leaving blanks for rest. At first he feels like he's slacking, but after a while quality improves, mistakes decrease, and he regains patience with his work.

concepts.dilemma: 團隊裡有人炫耀熬夜，小瑄也跟著拼，覺得這是「專業」。結果她身體開始出狀況，卻還不敢停。
concepts.solution: 她試著在專案初期就寫「工時與交付節點」，並加上趕工費。第一次她被說「你太硬了」，但她撐住，最後專案順利交付，她也第一次在不爆肝的狀態下完成大案。
concepts.dilemma_en: Someone on the team brags about all-nighters. Xiaoxuan follows suit, thinking this is 'professional.' Her body starts having problems, but she still doesn't dare stop.
concepts.solution_en: She tries writing 'work hours and delivery milestones' at the project's start, adding rush fees. The first time she's told 'you're too rigid,' but she holds firm. The project delivers successfully, and it's her first time completing a major project without burning out.

concepts.dilemma: 小田做設計，客戶常說「現在就要看到」。他一急就交半成品，客戶更愛插手，反而越改越亂。
concepts.solution: 他改成「24小時內給方向、72小時內給版本」的節奏。客戶起初不耐，但當阿倫用更清楚的節點交付，決策反而更快，回頭改稿也變少。
concepts.dilemma_en: Alun does design, clients often say 'we need to see it now.' When he rushes, he delivers half-finished work, clients interfere more, making things messier.
concepts.solution_en: He switches to a rhythm of 'direction within 24 hours, version within 72 hours.' Clients are initially impatient, but when Alun delivers at clearer milestones, decisions actually speed up and revision rounds decrease.

concepts.dilemma: 小瑜在機構工作，每天被要求填表、寫報告、追KPI，做設計的時間變很少。她開始懷疑自己是不是只剩行政。
concepts.solution: 她跟主管談「最小可行文檔」：只記決策、風險、交付。談判很難，她也被說「不夠配合」，但她用一個月示範：文件更少，專案反而更順，最後主管接受了新格式。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoyu works at an institution, asked daily to fill forms, write reports, track KPIs. Design time shrinks. She starts wondering if she's just administrative staff.
concepts.solution_en: She negotiates with her manager for 'minimum viable documentation': only recording decisions, risks, and deliverables. The negotiation is hard, she's told she's 'not cooperative enough,' but she demonstrates for a month: less documentation, smoother projects. Finally the manager accepts the new format.

concepts.dilemma: 小南做社區藝術計畫，補助單位問「你影響了幾個人？有沒有數字？」他明明知道影響很深，但一講數字就覺得自己在說謊。
concepts.solution: 他做「雙軌成果」：一頁數字＋兩頁故事與訪談。填表時很累，也不一定被懂，但當他拿著這份文件去談下一個合作，對方更快理解他做的是什麼，資源也更穩。
concepts.dilemma_en: Axiang does community art projects. The funding body asks 'How many people did you impact? Do you have numbers?' He knows the impact is deep, but talking numbers feels like lying.
concepts.solution_en: He creates 'dual-track results': one page of numbers + two pages of stories and interviews. Filling forms is exhausting and not always understood, but when he uses this document for next collaborations, partners understand what he does faster, and resources become more stable.

concepts.dilemma: 小蕙覺得「不被看見＝不存在」，每天盯著按讚數，心情被數字牽著走。她甚至開始只做會被分享的題材。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaohui feels 'Not seen = doesn't exist.' She stares at like counts daily, with her mood driven by numbers. She even starts only creating shareable content.
concepts.solution: 她把成功改成「每月完成一件自己滿意的作品＋寄一次電子報」。前兩個月她很空虛，但半年後她收到長信：有人因她的文章來委託，她第一次覺得被看見不是靠追熱度。
concepts.solution_en: She redefines success as 'completing one self-satisfying piece monthly + sending one newsletter.' The first two months feel empty, but after six months she gets a long letter: someone approached her for work because of her article. For the first time, she feels truly seen without chasing trends.

concepts.dilemma: 阿凱靠某社群平台接案，某天突然被限流，詢問量掉一半，他像被抽走地板。
concepts.dilemma_en: Akai relies on a social platform for projects. One day it suddenly gets shadowbanned, inquiry drops by half, and he feels like the floor's been pulled out.
concepts.solution: 他開始把客戶導向「表單＋名單」，每次合作都留下email。起初很慢，他也嫌麻煩，但一年後平台再變動時，他靠名單照樣能找回案源。
concepts.solution_en: He starts directing clients to a form + email list, collecting addresses with each collaboration. It's initially slow and tedious, but a year later when the platform changes, he can still find projects through his list.

concepts.dilemma: 小芸發現平台喜歡某種乾淨風格，她就一直做那套。流量確實上來了，但她越來越不認識自己的作品。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoyun discovers the platform favors a clean aesthetic style, so she keeps making that. Traffic indeed increases, but she increasingly doesn't recognize her own work.
concepts.solution: 她把作品分成「平台友善」與「個人研究」兩種，並固定每月公開一次研究。剛開始研究貼文很冷，但她慢慢吸引到真正理解她的人，後來接到更匹配的合作。
concepts.solution_en: She divides her work into 'platform-friendly' and 'personal research,' publicly sharing research monthly. Initial research posts get little engagement, but gradually she attracts people who truly understand her, leading to more aligned collaborations.

concepts.dilemma: 小傑被教「每個貼文都要導購」，他開始把朋友、觀眾都當成潛在客戶，講話也變得像廣告。結果互動變少，他更焦慮。
concepts.dilemma_en: Awen is taught 'Every post should drive sales.' He starts treating friends and audiences as potential customers, and his speech becomes ad-like. Interactions decrease and his anxiety grows.
concepts.solution: 他改成「社群不是漏斗，是共同體」：每月一次免費分享，但同時開放付費支持與清楚回饋。前期收入不穩，他也懷疑自己，但當支持者變成固定成員後，他的內容不再那麼用力推銷也能活下來。
concepts.solution_en: He reframes: 'Community isn't a funnel, it's a collective.' He offers free sharing monthly, while opening paid support and clear feedback channels. Early income is unstable, but when supporters become regular members, his content doesn't need hard-selling to sustain.

concepts.dilemma: 小敏想做一個需要時間看懂的作品，但朋友說「這不夠一眼懂」。她改成簡單版本後很快被分享，卻覺得自己背叛了初衷。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaomin wants to create work that requires time to understand, but friends say 'This isn't instantly graspable.' She changes to a simpler version and it quickly gets shared, but she feels she's betrayed her original intention.
concepts.solution: 她做「分層敘事」：短版讓人進來，長版留在網站/展場。短版做起來不難，但長版要寫、要整理，很花力氣；最後她發現真正的支持者會走到長版，她也找回作品的深度。
concepts.solution_en: She creates 'layered narrative': short version draws people in, full version lives on her website/exhibitions. The short version is easy, but the long version requires writing and organizing—lots of effort. Eventually she discovers true supporters will engage with the full version, reclaiming her work's depth.

concepts.dilemma: 品牌問阿杰「你的粉絲數多少？轉換率多少？」他明明做得很好，卻因為數字不大被否決。
concepts.dilemma_en: A brand asks Ajie 'How many followers? What's your conversion rate?' He's clearly doing well, but gets rejected for small numbers.
concepts.solution: 他用「可信任證據」替代：過往合作成果、流程可靠、推薦信。建立這套資料很費工，但當他拿這些去談中小型客戶，反而更快成交，因為對方要的是放心不是炫耀數字。
concepts.solution_en: He replaces this with 'credible evidence': past collaboration results, reliable process, recommendation letters. Building this is laborious, but when presenting to small-medium clients, deals close faster because they want reassurance, not flashy numbers.

concepts.dilemma: 小瑄常被要求「可以教一下怎麼做嗎」，她怕被說小氣就一直免費教。久了她發現自己像客服，創作時間被吃掉。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoxuan is constantly asked 'Can you teach me how to do this?' Afraid of being called stingy, she keeps teaching for free. Over time she realizes she's become customer service, with her creative time consumed.
concepts.solution: 她把教學分成三層：短答免費、系統教學收費、工作坊另計。她一開始很怕被討厭，但當她把規則講清楚，真正尊重她的人留下來，她也終於有時間做自己的作品。
concepts.solution_en: She divides teaching into three tiers: brief answers free, systematic teaching paid, workshops separate. At first she's afraid of being disliked, but when she makes the rules clear, those who truly respect her stay, and she finally has time for her own work.

concepts.dilemma: 阿德把作品都放在平台上，某天帳號出問題，作品像被鎖在別人家。
concepts.dilemma_en: Ade puts all his work on platforms. One day his account has issues, and his work is like being locked in someone else's house.
concepts.solution: 他每次發文都同步備份到自己的網站與硬碟。備份很無聊也很慢，但一年後他換平台時一點都不慌，甚至用自己的網站拿到更專業的合作。
concepts.solution_en: Every time he posts, he simultaneously backs up to his own website and hard drive. Backing up is tedious and slow, but a year later when he switches platforms, he's not panicked and even gets more professional collaborations through his own website.

concepts.dilemma: 小晴在公司提到可及性與勞動權益，被同事說「不要把政治帶進來」。她開始沉默，覺得自己的價值只能吞下去。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoqing mentions accessibility and labor rights at her company, colleagues tell her 'Don't bring politics into this.' She starts staying silent, feeling her values can only be swallowed.
concepts.solution: 她把「政治」翻成「風險與品質」：可及性＝更多用戶、勞動界線＝更少失誤。說服過程很累，也會被挑戰，但當一次專案因可及性做得好被客戶稱讚，公司才開始把這當成標準。
concepts.solution_en: She translates 'politics' into 'risk and quality': accessibility = more users, labor boundaries = fewer errors. The persuasion process is exhausting and gets challenged, but when a project receives client praise for good accessibility, the company finally treats it as standard.

concepts.dilemma: 阿恩每次展覽都被問「你這次有什麼新東西？」他把自己逼到每季都要換題目，最後只剩疲憊。
concepts.dilemma_en: Every exhibition, Aen is asked 'What's new this time?' He pushes himself to change topics every season, ending up exhausted.
concepts.solution: 他學會把同一計畫切成不同章節，讓長期研究也能被理解。觀眾一開始會覺得「怎麼還在做這個」，但當他把每章的差異與學到的東西說清楚，支持反而更深。
concepts.solution_en: He learns to divide the same project into different chapters, making long-term research understandable. Audiences initially think 'Still doing this?' but when he clearly explains the differences and learnings in each chapter, support actually deepens.

concepts.dilemma: 小琳在國際申請時被暗示「要更全球化、更普世」。她把在地語境削薄，結果作品像觀光紀念品。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaolin is hinted during international applications to 'be more global, more universal.' She dilutes local context, and her work becomes like a tourist souvenir.
concepts.solution: 她保留語境的複雜度，但加上互譯：讓外地人看得懂、又不把自己變成被消費的符號。翻譯很辛苦也常被誤解，但當她找到願意共同策展的夥伴後，作品在國際場合反而更站得住。
concepts.solution_en: She keeps contextual complexity but adds inter-translation: letting outsiders understand without becoming a consumed symbol. Translation is hard and often misunderstood, but when she finds partners willing to co-curate, her work stands stronger internationally.

concepts.dilemma: 小平做批判作品，機構邀展卻只想用他的政治性當宣傳。展完之後機構照樣不改內部不公平，他覺得被利用。
concepts.dilemma_en: Ahao makes critical work. Institutions invite his exhibitions but only want to use his politics for publicity. After the show, the institution doesn't change internal inequities, and he feels exploited.
concepts.solution: 他開始提出合作條件：酬勞、話語權、社群回饋、公開對話。談判常失敗，他也會失去舞台，但當一個較小的空間接受條款，展覽變成真的對話，他才覺得作品沒有被吞掉。
concepts.solution_en: He starts proposing collaboration terms: compensation, voice, community feedback, public dialogue. Negotiations often fail and he loses platforms, but when a smaller venue accepts the terms, the exhibition becomes real dialogue, and he feels his work isn't swallowed.

concepts.dilemma: 小真每年投補助，總覺得「名額很少，我要拼命」。她把時間花在寫申請，作品反而停住。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaozhen applies for grants yearly, always feeling 'Few slots, I must fight hard.' She spends time writing applications while her work actually stalls.
concepts.solution: 她做混合收入：一部分補助、一部分教學、一部分小額訂閱。起初每一條都很小很不穩，但半年後三條加起來形成底，她投補助時也不再像在賭命。
concepts.solution_en: She creates mixed income: part grants, part teaching, part small subscriptions. Initially each stream is small and unstable, but after six months the three combined form a foundation, and she no longer feels like betting her life on grants.

concepts.dilemma: 阿哲把一整個月都用來投件，填表填到懷疑人生。最後沒上，他像白做工一樣崩潰。
concepts.dilemma_en: Azhe spends an entire month submitting applications, filling forms until questioning his sanity. When he doesn't get in, he collapses like he worked for nothing.
concepts.solution: 他加入「投件互助」：共用資料庫、互改、分工整理素材。協作一開始會混亂，但當大家建立模板後，投件時間少一半，上了的人也會回饋資源，整體更不孤單。
concepts.solution_en: He joins an 'application mutual aid' group: shared database, peer editing, collaborative material organization. Collaboration is initially messy, but when everyone builds templates, application time halves, and successful members share resources back, making everything less lonely.

concepts.dilemma: 小晴被邀請做活動視覺，「我們可以給你曝光」。她答應了，結果忙一週只換到一句Tag，帳單照樣來。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoxin is invited to do event visuals. They say, 'We can give you exposure.' She agrees, spending a week working for just a tag mention while bills still come due.
concepts.solution: 她改成「曝光可以，但至少要交通＋製作費」，並要求對方提供可驗證的曝光方式（名單、連結、回饋）。一開始她被說現實，但當她不再倒貼，反而有活動願意把她當真正的合作方。
concepts.solution_en: She switches to 'Exposure is possible, but at least cover transport + production costs' and requires verifiable exposure methods (lists, links, feedback). Initially she's called mercenary, but when she stops subsidizing, events actually start treating her as a real partner.

concepts.dilemma: 阿凱發現很多機會只在某些圈子流通，他不在那裡就永遠晚一步。
concepts.dilemma_en: Akai discovers that many opportunities only circulate within certain networks. If he's not in those circles, he's always one step behind.
concepts.solution: 他開始參與公開社群、建立公開機會板，並把自己知道的也分享出去。這種做法短期不會讓他立刻上位，但長期讓他成為「資訊可靠的人」，合作與機會反而更常回到他身上。
concepts.solution_en: He starts participating in open communities, building public opportunity boards, and sharing what he knows. This doesn't immediately elevate him, but long-term he becomes 'the reliable information person,' and collaborations and opportunities more often come his way.

concepts.dilemma: 小慧的工作其實包含協調、研究、照護團隊，但面試只看作品圖。她覺得自己做的那些都像不存在。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaohui's work actually involves coordination, research, and team care, but interviews only look at portfolio images. She feels like everything she does doesn't count.
concepts.solution: 她做「案例敘事」：用一頁寫清楚她解決了什麼問題、怎麼協作、如何降低風險。寫起來很痛苦也很花時間，但當她遇到懂得看「工作方法」的雇主，對方反而更快信任她。
concepts.solution_en: She creates 'case narratives': one-page documents that clearly explain what problems she solved, how she collaborated, and how she mitigated risks. Writing these is painful and time-consuming, but when she meets employers who understand 'working methods,' they trust her faster.

concepts.dilemma: 阿文被要求「先變成可雇用的人」，他把自己改成一套標準模板。工作找到了，但他每天都覺得自己像被借走的人。
concepts.dilemma_en: Awen is told to 'first become an employable person.' He remolds himself into a standard template. He finds a job, but every day feels like he's borrowed.
concepts.solution: 他保留一個「不為雇用而做」的小計畫，每週固定兩小時。剛開始很難、也常被加班吞掉，但他慢慢用這個計畫建立下一步的方向，最後轉到更適合他的團隊。
concepts.solution_en: He keeps a small 'non-employment project' with fixed two hours per week. Initially it's difficult and often consumed by overtime, but gradually he uses this project to chart his next direction, eventually moving to a team that suits him better.

concepts.dilemma: 小倩做行銷設計，被要求加更多追蹤碼、蒐集更多資料。她覺得不舒服，但又怕說了會被當成不懂商業。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoqian does marketing design and is asked to add more tracking codes and collect more data. She's uncomfortable but fears being seen as not understanding business.
concepts.solution: 她提出「最少數據方案」：只追必要指標、明確告知使用者。推動很難，主管一開始覺得她找麻煩，但當隱私議題成為風險，團隊反而需要她這種人，最後她也被視為能控風險的專業。
concepts.solution_en: She proposes a 'minimal data approach': track only necessary metrics and clearly inform users. It's difficult to push through; her manager initially thinks she's creating problems. But when privacy becomes a risk issue, the team needs people like her, and she's eventually seen as someone who can manage risk.

concepts.dilemma: 阿哲遠端工作被要求開螢幕監控，連休息都怕被記錄。他越想證明自己努力，越做不出好東西。
concepts.dilemma_en: Azhe works remotely and is required to have screen monitoring. Even rest time makes him anxious about being tracked. The harder he tries to prove he's working, the worse his output becomes.
concepts.solution: 他跟團隊提議改為「成果里程碑」：每週交付清單、不要監控過程。談判會被懷疑、也可能失敗，但當他拿出兩週的成果對比（監控下更慢），主管才願意試行，最後效率真的提高。
concepts.solution_en: He proposes changing to 'outcome milestones' to his team: weekly deliverables, no process monitoring. The negotiation faces skepticism and could fail, but when he shows two weeks of output comparison (slower under monitoring), his manager agrees to try it, and efficiency actually improves.

concepts.dilemma: 小芸在家工作，客戶和同事隨時找她，她覺得自己像永遠待命。久了她連放鬆都不會。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoyun works from home and clients and colleagues reach her anytime. She feels perpetually on call. Eventually she forgets how to relax.
concepts.solution: 她設「數位宵禁」：晚上不回訊息，緊急用電話且要付費。前期她非常不安，但當她穩住界線後，白天更專注，交付更快，反而讓人更尊重她的時間。
concepts.solution_en: She sets a 'digital curfew': no responses after hours, emergencies require a phone call at premium rate. Initially she's very anxious, but once she stabilizes the boundary, daytime focus improves, deliverables speed up, and people actually respect her time more.

concepts.dilemma: 阿凱一直追新軟體，覺得不用就落伍。結果他學一堆功能卻沒有任何作品落地。
concepts.dilemma_en: Akai constantly chases new software, afraid of falling behind if he doesn't use it. Result: he learns lots of features but never completes any actual work.
concepts.solution: 他選「最小工具鏈」：只保留兩個核心工具，其他一年只評估一次。放下潮流很難，他會手癢，但當作品開始真正完成，他才發現「完成」比「會很多」更有力量。
concepts.solution_en: He chooses a 'minimal tool chain': keeps only two core tools, evaluates others once a year. Letting go of trends is hard and he gets itchy, but when work actually starts completing, he discovers that 'finishing' is more powerful than 'knowing many.'

concepts.dilemma: 公司說AI讓你更快，所以你要接更多工作。小晴越做越快，卻越覺得自己像機器。
concepts.dilemma_en: The company says AI makes you faster, so take on more work. Xiaoqing works faster and faster, but increasingly feels like a machine.
concepts.solution: 她把AI用在「省掉重複勞動」，把省下的時間用在研究、提案、品質控制。主管起初只想榨更多量，但當她用更好的成果說服客戶加價，公司才接受「快不是為了更多量，是為了更好」。
concepts.solution_en: She uses AI to 'eliminate repetitive work' and spends saved time on research, proposals, quality control. Her manager initially wants to squeeze more output, but when she uses better results to convince clients to pay more, the company accepts that 'faster is not for more quantity, but better quality.'

concepts.dilemma: 阿文看到有人用超低價接案，客戶也拿來壓他。他一降價就要加班補回來，變成惡性循環。
concepts.dilemma_en: Awen sees people bidding at rock-bottom prices, and clients use that to pressure him. Every time he cuts price, he must work overtime to compensate, creating a vicious circle.
concepts.solution: 他改成賣「清楚成果」：交付範圍、修改次數、風險控制，並用案例證明省下客戶時間。前期會被嫌貴，但留下的客戶更長期，他也不必用命換錢。
concepts.solution_en: He shifts to selling 'clear outcomes': defined deliverables, revision limits, risk management, and case studies proving he saves client time. Early he'll be called expensive, but remaining clients stay longer and he doesn't have to trade health for money.

concepts.dilemma: 有人對小真說「市場就這樣，價值就是看誰願意付」。她聽久了覺得自己只能接受，不可能改變。
concepts.dilemma_en: Someone tells Xiaozhen 'that's just how markets are; value is whatever someone will pay.' Hearing this long enough, she feels she can only accept it, that change is impossible.
concepts.solution: 她開始參與在地市集與公共合作，把價值放回「使用者需要什麼」。這條路不會爆紅，也常遇到資源不足，但她慢慢建立固定社群，收入不一定最高，卻更穩也更有尊嚴。
concepts.solution_en: She starts participating in local markets and public collaborations, refocusing value on 'what users actually need.' This path won't go viral and often faces resource shortage, but she gradually builds a stable community. Income isn't the highest, but it's more reliable and dignified.

concepts.dilemma: 阿豪本來做很私人的作品，後來為了賣，把情緒也包裝成商品。賣出去那一刻他不開心，反而像失去一部分自己。
concepts.dilemma_en: Aohao originally made personal work, but to sell, he packaged his emotions as merchandise too. When he sold, he wasn't happy; it felt like losing part of himself.
concepts.solution: 他把作品分成「商品」與「不賣的作品」。一開始他會怕不賣就浪費，但當他保留一塊不被交易的空間，創作動力回來了，商品線也更健康，因為不是用痛苦硬擠。
concepts.solution_en: He divides his work into 'commercial' and 'unsellable pieces.' At first he fears unsold work is waste, but by keeping a non-commodified space, creative drive returns, and his commercial line becomes healthier because it's not forced from pain.

concepts.dilemma: 小雅連旅行都在想「能不能拍成內容」。她拍到了，但整趟旅程像在上班，回家更空。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoya even wonders 'can I footage this?' during travel. She gets the content, but the whole trip feels like work, and returning home feels emptier.
concepts.solution: 她設定「不可變現日」：那天不拍、不發、不想業配。剛開始她怕錯過機會，但長期下來她更有能量，也更能做出真正想分享的東西。
concepts.solution_en: She designates 'non-monetizable days': no filming, no posting, no sponsored content. At first she fears missing opportunities, but over time she has more energy and can create things she genuinely wants to share.

concepts.dilemma: 阿哲一直用「我是不是值得投資」看自己，做任何決定都像在算報酬率。他開始不敢冒險，也不敢停。
concepts.dilemma_en: Azhe always evaluates himself as 'am I investable?' Every decision feels like calculating ROI. He becomes afraid to take risks or stop.
concepts.solution: 他改用「我在服務誰、解決什麼」來做選擇。轉念很難，因為焦慮會一直回來，但當他用這個標準找到更一致的工作與社群，他的自我價值感不再每天被市場價格波動。
concepts.solution_en: He shifts to asking 'who am I serving? what problem do I solve?' to make decisions. Reframing is hard because anxiety keeps returning, but when he uses this standard to find more consistent work and community, his self-worth stops fluctuating with market prices daily.

concepts.dilemma: 小芸看到同行得案子就嫉妒，還會偷偷希望對方出錯。她知道這樣很糟，但停不下來。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoyun envies peers when they get projects, even secretly hoping they fail. She knows it's unhealthy but can't stop.
concepts.solution: 她開始做「互推」：把不適合自己的案子轉給別人，別人也回轉。起初她怕自己吃虧，但當這種互惠形成圈，她反而更常接到剛好適合她的案子，心態也變健康。
concepts.solution_en: She starts 'cross-recommending': referring projects unsuitable for her to others, and they reciprocate. At first she fears losing out, but when reciprocity forms a network, she receives better-fitting projects more often, and her mindset improves.

concepts.dilemma: 阿文加入社群本來想交朋友，最後卻變成一直在找案子、看誰有資源。朋友像資源，資源像朋友，他越來越累。
concepts.dilemma_en: Awen joined a community to make friends, but it became case-hunting and resource-tracking. Friends become resources, resources become friends, he gets exhausted.
concepts.solution: 他把社群分成兩個：一個是純朋友不談工作，一個是合作社群談規則。切分一開始很彆扭，但久了他的關係更清楚、也更不會在每次聊天時都感到壓力。
concepts.solution_en: He splits communities: one for friends only without work talk, one for collaboration with clear rules. Initial separation feels awkward, but relationships become clearer and conversations stop feeling pressured.

concepts.dilemma: 小倩一邊照顧家人一邊接案，總覺得自己效率差。她看到別人全力衝刺，就覺得自己「不夠專業」。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoqian freelances while caring for family, always feeling inefficient. Seeing peers full-steam-ahead, she feels 'not professional enough.'
concepts.solution: 她開始把照護時間納入排程與報價，接更少案子但更合理。前期收入會緊，她也會內疚，但當她找到願意尊重時程的客戶後，生活與工作終於不再互相摧毀。
concepts.solution_en: She factorizes care time into scheduling and pricing, taking fewer projects but with realistic terms. Early income tightens and guilt arises, but once she finds clients respecting her timeline, life and work stop destroying each other.

concepts.dilemma: 阿豪總把自己放最後，覺得顧好別人才是好人。久了他心裡很怨，工作也開始敷衍。
concepts.dilemma_en: Aohao always puts himself last, believing taking care of others makes him good. Over time he resents, and work becomes perfunctory.
concepts.solution: 他把照護改成「有界線的照護」：先照顧自己，再照顧他人。這不是一夜之間做到，他會反覆破功，但當他能說「我現在不行」而不崩潰時，他才真正變得可靠。
concepts.solution_en: He reframes care as 'bounded care': self-care first, then others. It doesn't happen overnight and he backslides, but when he can say 'I can't right now' without breaking, he becomes truly reliable.

concepts.dilemma: 朋友介紹案子，對方說「我們熟，不用合約」。小雅相信了，結果對方一直改、一直拖，最後還砍價。
concepts.dilemma_en: A friend introduces a project, saying 'we're close, no contract needed.' Xiaoya believes, but the client keeps revising, delaying, and finally cuts price.
concepts.solution: 她用「最小合約」：一頁紙寫範圍、時間、付款。對方一開始酸她太見外，但她堅持：合約是保護關係，不是破壞關係；最後合作反而更順，朋友也沒因此消失。
concepts.solution_en: She uses a 'minimal contract': one page for scope, timeline, payment. Initially the client calls her cold, but she insists: contracts protect relationships, they don't harm them. Collaboration actually runs smoother and the friendship persists.

concepts.dilemma: 客戶對阿文說「我給你機會耶」，讓他不敢談條件。做完後對方還說「你要感恩」。
concepts.dilemma_en: A client tells Awen 'I'm giving you an opportunity,' making him afraid to negotiate. After completion, they say 'you should be grateful.'
concepts.solution: 他學會回應：「謝謝信任，我也會用專業交付，所以我們把條件講清楚。」第一次說出口很難，他聲音都抖，但當他把互利講成自然的事，對方反而比較不敢亂來。
concepts.solution_en: He learns to respond: 'Thanks for the trust, and I'll deliver professionally, so let's clarify terms.' Saying it first time is hard, voice shaking, but when he frames reciprocity naturally, the client behaves better.

concepts.dilemma: 小倩做設計，甲方內部吵架，最後把責任推到她身上：「你怎麼沒提醒？」
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoqian designs, the client's internal team argues, then blames her: 'Why didn't you warn us?'
concepts.solution: 她開始每次都用「確認點」鎖決策：哪個版本誰同意、什麼時間點決定。寫紀錄很煩，但當糾紛來時，她能拿出文件保護自己，專案也更少內耗。
concepts.solution_en: She uses 'confirmation checkpoints' to lock decisions: which version, who approved, when. Documenting is tedious, but when disputes arise, she has records to protect herself, and projects have fewer conflicts.

concepts.dilemma: 阿豪的案子有五個人給意見，每個人都要改，最後變成四不像。
concepts.dilemma_en: Aohao's project has five people giving input, each demanding changes, result looks frankly incoherent.
concepts.solution: 他要求對方指定一位決策者，其他人只能在某個時間點一次給意見。對方一開始覺得他很強勢，但當改稿次數大幅下降、交付更快，他們反而願意接受這種秩序。
concepts.solution_en: He requests one designated decision maker; others provide feedback at specific checkpoints only. Client initially finds him demanding, but when revisions drop sharply and delivery quickens, they embrace this structure.

concepts.dilemma: 小雅的專案永遠「差一點」，客戶一直說再優化一下。她不敢結案，錢也收不齊。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoya's projects are never 'fully done.' Clients keep saying 'optimize a bit more.' She can't close and can't collect full payment.
concepts.solution: 她在一開始寫清楚「完成＝哪些檔案＋哪些頁面＋幾次修改」。客戶會想留模糊空間，她得一直拉回條款；但當她能準時結案並把維護另約，收入與生活都更穩。
concepts.solution_en: She clearly defines upfront: 'Done = these files + these pages + X revisions.' Clients try to keep it fuzzy, she must repeatedly reference terms, but when she closes on time and separates maintenance, income and life stabilize.

concepts.dilemma: 阿哲每月收入像雲霄飛車，忙時忙到爆，閒時閒到怕。
concepts.dilemma_en: Azhe's monthly income is a rollercoaster: overwhelming when busy, terrifying when slow.
concepts.solution: 他向舊客戶推「每月固定時數」：小額但穩定。客戶起初會猶豫，他也怕被拒絕，但當第一個客戶買了，其他客戶也更願意跟進，他的焦慮像被放下了一半。
concepts.solution_en: He pitches 'monthly fixed hours' to returning clients: small but stable. Clients hesitate initially and he fears rejection, but once one buys, others follow, and his anxiety halves.

concepts.dilemma: 小倩不知道行情，只能憑感覺報價，常常報太低還以為是自己不夠格。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoqian doesn't know market rates, quotes by feel, often too low and assumes it's her lack of quality.
concepts.solution: 她跟同業交換費率，建立匿名共享表。有人會怕被比較、怕被討厭，但當她拿到更接近真實的區間，她談價更有底氣，也更不容易被壓。
concepts.solution_en: She exchanges rates with peers, creates anonymous shared spreadsheet. Some fear comparison or resentment, but with realistic ranges, she negotiates confidently and resists pressure.

concepts.dilemma: 阿豪一個人去跟大客戶談條件，對方一句「我們很多人排隊」就把他壓回去。
concepts.dilemma_en: Aohao negotiates alone with a major client; one sentence 'many people want your position' and he's pressured into submission.
concepts.solution: 他和幾個同行約定一起守底線，必要時一起拒絕不合理條款。這需要高度信任，也會有人想偷跑，但當客戶發現「不是一個人難搞，是整個圈子有標準」，條件才開始鬆動。
concepts.solution_en: He and peers agree to hold a line together, refusing unreasonable terms if needed. This requires deep trust and some may breach, but when clients discover 'it's not one person being difficult, it's industry standards,' terms actually improve.

concepts.dilemma: 小雅不想當老闆也不想一直單飛，但找工作又覺得被綁死。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoya doesn't want to be a boss or freelance forever, but employment feels suffocating.
concepts.solution: 她和兩個夥伴成立小合作社：共同接案、共同分配、共同決策。前期最難是溝通與錢的分配，磨合會很痛，但當規則跑順後，她既有團隊支撐又保有自主。
concepts.solution_en: She and two partners form a small co-op: joint client work, shared revenue, collective decisions. Hardest early is communication and money splits, bonding is painful, but once systems smooth, she has team support and autonomy.

concepts.dilemma: 阿哲突然生病，案子停擺，房租快繳不出來。他覺得向人求助很丟臉。
concepts.dilemma_en: Azhe suddenly falls ill, work stops, rent comes due. He's ashamed to ask for help.
concepts.solution: 他加入互助網：急難支援、轉介案子、共享資源。求助那刻很難，但當他康復後也回去幫別人，他才明白互助不是施捨，是讓每個人都能撐過低谷。
concepts.solution_en: He joins a mutual aid network: emergency funds, referrals, shared resources. Asking is hard, but after recovering and helping others, he learns mutual aid isn't charity; it's how everyone survives difficult times.

concepts.dilemma: 小倩做的設計明明改善了可及性與理解，但客戶只看短期轉換率，說她做的「不划算」。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoqian's design improves accessibility and understanding, but clients only see short-term conversion rates and call it 'not worthwhile.'
concepts.solution: 她把公共價值翻成可理解的語言：減少客服成本、增加信任、提升回訪。推這套需要時間與證據，她也會被說太理想，但當一次專案因為信任提升而帶來長期客戶，對方才開始願意聽。
concepts.solution_en: She translates public value into business language: reduced support costs, increased trust, higher retention. Pushing this takes time and data, and she's called idealistic, but when one project brings long-term clients through trust, she has proof.

concepts.dilemma: 阿豪一直追「看起來很酷」的案子，作品很漂亮，卻常被使用者說「看不懂、不好用」。
concepts.dilemma_en: Aohao chases 'cool-looking' projects; his work is beautiful but users say 'don't understand, hard to use.'
concepts.solution: 他把設計的第一問題改成「誰要用？什麼時候用？」做更多小測試。測試會被嫌慢，也會遇到沒預算，但當他把錯誤在早期修掉，專案後期就不再炸裂。
concepts.solution_en: He reframes the first question to 'who uses this? when?' and does more testing. Testing seems slow and budgets are tight, but catching errors early prevents project meltdowns.

concepts.dilemma: 小雅每次談合約就頭痛，覺得寫太多會嚇跑人，結果常被拖延與追加。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoya dreads contracts, thinks writing much scares people, instead gets delays and scope creep.
concepts.solution: 她做一份一頁模板：範圍、時間、付款、修改、版權。有人仍會嫌麻煩，她也要練習「不簽就不開工」，但當她遇到好客戶，對方反而覺得她專業可靠。
concepts.solution_en: She creates a one-page template: scope, timeline, payment, revisions, copyright. Some still complain, she learns 'no signature, no start,' but good clients find it professional.

concepts.dilemma: 阿哲每次客戶說「順便改一下」，他就答應，最後整個案子被順便改到失控。
concepts.dilemma_en: Azhe agrees every time a client says 'just tweak this,' ending with scope spiraling out of control.
concepts.solution: 他建立一句固定回應：「可以，我整理成變更單給你選。」客戶會不爽、他也會怕，但當對方必須在「要不要加錢」之間做選擇，無限追加才停得下來。
concepts.solution_en: He has a standard response: 'Sure, I'll document it as a change order for approval.' Clients get frustrated, he's nervous, but when they must choose 'pay extra or not,' scope creep stops.

concepts.dilemma: 小倩每次遇到急件都硬吞，結果生活被打爛，還被當作理所當然。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoqian absorbs every rush job, her life implodes, clients expect it as normal.
concepts.solution: 她設加急費並寫清楚「加急會影響其他案子」。第一次收加急費她很不安，但當客戶真的付了，她才知道急不是你的責任，是對方的需求要付代價。
concepts.solution_en: She sets a rush fee and notes 'rush work affects other projects.' First rush fee makes her nervous, but when clients actually pay, she learns: urgency isn't her burden, it's their cost.

concepts.dilemma: 阿豪的團隊天天即時會議，會越開越多，事情反而越慢。
concepts.dilemma_en: Aohao's team has daily real-time meetings, growing more frequent, work actually slows down.
concepts.solution: 他推行非同步：文件先寫、會議只做決策。改文化會被抵抗，也會有人偷懶不寫，但當團隊發現會議變少、決策更快，大家才願意守規則。
concepts.solution_en: He implements async: document first, meetings for decisions only. Culture change faces resistance, some avoid documenting, but when meetings shrink and decisions speed up, people embrace it.

concepts.dilemma: 小雅想做無障礙，但被說「那是加分項，不是必要」。她覺得自己在做沒人看見的苦工。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoya wants to do accessibility work, but hears 'that's a bonus, not required.' She feels like she's doing invisible labor.
concepts.solution: 她把可及性變成流程的一部分，並把風險說清楚：不做可能會失去使用者、甚至出法律問題。推動很辛苦，但當一次活動網站因無障礙做得好被大量轉傳，客戶才開始把它當必要。
concepts.solution_en: She makes accessibility part of process and articulates risks: loss of users, legal exposure. Advocating is hard, but when one event website goes viral for best accessibility, clients recognize it as essential.

concepts.dilemma: 阿哲接到大品牌合作，但品牌價值觀跟他相反。錢很誘人，他也很矛盾。
concepts.dilemma_en: Azhe gets offered a major brand partnership, but the brand's values contradict his. Money is tempting, he's conflicted.
concepts.solution: 他做「拒絕清單」與「可談條件」：例如要透明揭露、要回饋社群。拒絕會痛、也會怕錯失，但當他守住底線後，他吸引到更一致的合作方，長期更安心。
concepts.solution_en: He makes a 'no-list' and 'negotiable terms': transparency required, community benefit mandatory. Rejecting hurts and he fears missing out, but maintaining limits attracts aligned sponsors and provide long-term peace.

concepts.dilemma: 小倩被邀展，機構說「你來就好，其他我們決定」。她擔心自己只變成裝飾。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoqian is invited to exhibit; institution says 'just show up, we decide everything.' She fears being decoration.
concepts.solution: 她要求條件：製作費、協作方式、對話空間。談判會被說難搞，但當機構接受一部分，她的作品在展場不只是被展示，也真的引發討論與回饋。
concepts.solution_en: She requests conditions: production budget, collaboration process, dialogue space. Negotiating seems difficult, but when institutions accept, her work inspires discussions and feedback, not just hanging on walls.

concepts.dilemma: 阿豪做了大量工作，最後成品只寫了主管名字。別人以為他只是執行。
concepts.dilemma_en: Aohao does substantial work but only his manager is credited. Others assume he's just execution.
concepts.solution: 他在合作前就把署名寫進文件，並在交付時附上貢獻清單。提出署名很尷尬也可能得罪人，但當他累積幾次清楚署名，外界才開始正確看見他的能力。
concepts.solution_en: He documents credits upfront and provides contribution lists at delivery. Requesting credits is awkward and may offend, but consistent clear crediting eventually shows his real capabilities to the world.

concepts.dilemma: 小雅覺得文化圈總說「大家都很窮」，所以低薪是常態。她每次接受一次，心裡就更麻木。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoya hears the creative world say 'everyone is broke,' making low pay normal. Each acceptance numbs her more.
concepts.solution: 她跟同業一起做「最低報酬倡議」：公開費率、拒接剝削案。推動很慢，也會被說破壞氣氛，但當一些活動開始願意先把酬勞列入預算，標準就真的被推動了一點點。
concepts.solution_en: She and peers run a 'minimum compensation initiative': publish rates, refuse exploitative work. Change is slow and she's called divisive, but when events start budgeting pay first, standards actually shift.

concepts.dilemma: 阿哲一直追大城市大平台，覺得那才算成功，結果身心耗盡，作品也離生活很遠。
concepts.dilemma_en: Azhe chases big cities and big platforms, thinking that's success; he's exhausted and his work feels remote from life.
concepts.solution: 他把一部分工作放回在地：和社區、學校、店家合作。收入起初不高、資源也少，但因為關係更穩，他反而做出更扎實的作品，生活也更能被支撐。
concepts.solution_en: He brings work back local: collaborating with communities, schools, shops. Early income is low and resources scarce, but stable relationships enable better work and his life feels supported.

concepts.dilemma: 小倩覺得一定要擴張才安全，於是接更多案、找更多客戶，結果管理成本炸裂。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoqian believes expansion is security; she takes more projects, seeks more clients, management costs explode.
concepts.solution: 她把目標改成「剛剛好能活、能休息、能創作」。放下擴張會焦慮，但當她算清楚生活成本與工作上限，她的公司變小卻更健康，反而更長久。
concepts.solution_en: She redefines success as 'good enough to live, rest, and create.' Letting go of expansion triggers anxiety, but when she calculates true costs and capacity, her smaller business is healthier and lasts longer.

concepts.dilemma: 阿豪把每個想法都拿去變現，久了腦袋只剩「有沒有市場」。他開始感覺自己乾掉了。
concepts.dilemma_en: Aohao monetizes every idea; eventually his mind only asks 'is there a market?' He feels himself drying up.
concepts.solution: 他保留一塊「不賣、不交付、不討好」的時間。初期他會懷疑這是不是浪費，但當那塊空間長出新作品與新方向，他才知道這是在養根。
concepts.solution_en: He protects time that's 'non-commercial, unshipped, no approval needed.' Initially he questions if it's wasted, but when that space generates new work and directions, he understands it's cultivation.

concepts.dilemma: 小雅做的議題很複雜，常被說太難懂。她要嘛被迫簡化，要嘛被忽略。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoya works on complex topics, often told 'too hard to understand.' She either oversimplifies or is ignored.
concepts.solution: 她做三層：一句話、三段話、完整版。做完整版很累、也需要寫作練習，但當她把入口做得友善，更多人願意走進深處，她也不用背叛複雜度。
concepts.solution_en: She creates three layers: one sentence, three paragraphs, full version. Writing complete versions is tiring and requires skill, but friendly entry points let more people dive deeper without betraying nuance.

concepts.dilemma: 阿哲總覺得把案子轉出去很可惜，所以硬吃不適合的案。結果客戶不滿意，他也很痛苦。
concepts.dilemma_en: Azhe feels referrals are lost opportunities, so he takes unsuitable projects. Clients aren't happy, he suffers.
concepts.solution: 他開始把不適合的案子轉給更適合的人，並建立互推回流。前期他怕自己吃虧，但當別人也把適合他的案子轉回來，工作變得更順，口碑也更好。
concepts.solution_en: He refers unsuitable projects to better-fit people and builds reciprocal loops. Initially he fears losing out, but when peers send matching work back, his workflow improves and reputation grows.

concepts.dilemma: 小倩只靠單一收入（接案），一旦市場冷就立刻恐慌。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoqian relies solely on project income; market slowdown triggers immediate panic.
concepts.solution: 她把收入拆成三條：接案、教學、授權/小商品。每條都要時間建，初期很累、像三份工作，但當三條一起跑起來，她不再被單一市場綁架。
concepts.solution_en: She splits revenue into three streams: projects, teaching, licensing/products. Each takes time to build and early on feels like three jobs, but once all run together, she's not enslaved to one market.

concepts.dilemma: 阿豪以為「買斷」才專業，結果作品被用到天邊，他卻拿不到任何延伸收益。
concepts.dilemma_en: Aohao thought 'buyout' was professional; his work circulates forever but he gets no extension revenue.
concepts.solution: 他學會授權：限定用途、延展加價。談授權會被嫌麻煩，但當他遇到懂規則的客戶，合作反而更長期，因為雙方都知道怎麼用、怎麼付。
concepts.solution_en: He learns licensing: limit usage, charge for extensions. Negotiating licenses seems tedious, but clients who understand rules become long-term partners because both sides know terms.

concepts.dilemma: 小雅和朋友一起做團隊，沒有規則，最後誰做最多誰最累。久了感情也受傷。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoya and friends run a team with no rules; whoever does most gets most tired. Friendships suffer over time.
concepts.solution: 她們一起訂規則：工時、分帳、決策方式、衝突處理。建立規則會吵架，但當規則存在，爭執反而變少，合作也更能長久。
concepts.solution_en: They establish rules together: hours, revenue split, decision-making, conflict handling. Setting rules causes arguments, but with rules, disputes shrink and collaborations last longer.

concepts.dilemma: 阿哲不想只為品牌服務，但又怕公共案子沒錢、流程很煩。
concepts.dilemma_en: Azhe doesn't want to serve brands alone, but fears public work lacks budget and has bureaucracy.
concepts.solution: 他先從小型公共合作開始，學會怎麼寫提案與控風險。流程真的慢、文件很煩，但當他完成第一個公共案子後，後續資源與信任更容易累積，不再只是靠市場熱度。
concepts.solution_en: He starts with small public collaborations, learns proposal writing and risk management. Processes are slow and paperwork tedious, but after the first public project, resources and trust accumulate; he's not dependent on market heat.

concepts.dilemma: 小倩做產品設計，被要求蒐集更多資料以便「優化」。她心裡不安，但又怕被說不懂商業。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoqian designs products, is pressured to collect more data to 'optimize.' She's uncomfortable but fears being called commercial-illiterate.
concepts.solution: 她提出最小化方案：只收必要資料並清楚告知。推動會遇到阻力，但當她用「信任＝長期轉換」說服，團隊發現用戶反感下降，反而對產品更忠誠。
concepts.solution_en: She proposes minimization: collect only necessary data with clear disclosure. Pushback comes, but when she argues 'trust equals retention,' the team discovers user resistance drops and loyalty increases.

concepts.dilemma: 阿豪交付後客戶說「有問題都是你負責」，但很多問題其實是客戶自行改動造成。
concepts.dilemma_en: Aohao delivers and clients say 'all problems are your responsibility,' but many stem from their own changes.
concepts.solution: 他加上責任界定：你改我就不保固、保固範圍清楚寫。客戶會不爽，但當他把界線說得很專業，真正想長期合作的客戶反而更安心。
concepts.solution_en: He defines liability: if you modify, warranty voids; warranty scope is clear. Clients resist, but when boundaries are articulated professionally, serious partners feel safer and more aligned.

concepts.dilemma: 小雅遇到一直不付款的客戶，卻仍被催交付。她越做越委屈，還怕得罪人。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoya works for a client who won't pay but demands delivery. She gets more hurt and fears offending them.
concepts.solution: 她設定逾期就停工並書面通知。第一次停工她很害怕，但當她真的執行，對方才開始認真付款；她也第一次知道自己可以保護自己。
concepts.solution_en: She sets payment-due stop-work with formal notice. First stop-work scares her, but once executed, the client pays seriously; she learns she can protect herself.

concepts.dilemma: 阿哲報價只算工時，沒算溝通、管理、修改，結果做完覺得自己被掏空。
concepts.dilemma_en: Azhe prices only labor hours, not communication, management, revisions; finishing leaves him depleted.
concepts.solution: 他把「不可見工」列進報價：溝通、管理、風險。報價變高會被拒絕，他也會焦慮，但當他找到願意付真成本的客戶，工作才不再是慢性倒貼。
concepts.solution_en: He includes 'invisible work' in pricing: communication, management, contingency. Quotes rise and rejections increase, but clients paying true costs mean work stops being slow theft.

concepts.dilemma: 小倩的時間被別人的需求切碎，永遠在回訊息，做不了深度工作。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoqian's time is fragmentized by others' needs, always messaging, can't do deep work.
concepts.solution: 她把時間切成「深度區塊」並關通知。剛開始她怕錯過急事，但當她設定緊急通道（電話＋加急費），她反而更能掌控節奏，也交付得更好。
concepts.solution_en: She creates 'deep work blocks' and disables notifications. Initially she fears missing emergencies, but when she establishes emergency channels (phone + rush fee), she controls pacing better and delivers stronger work.

concepts.dilemma: 阿豪每天刷平台找靈感，最後變成比較與焦慮，腦袋像被占滿。
concepts.dilemma_en: Aohao scrolls platforms daily for inspiration; he becomes comparative and anxious, mind feels colonized.
concepts.solution: 他設「攝取窗口」：每天只在固定時間看，其他時間只做。戒斷期很痛，他會手癢，但當他重新把注意力放回作品，他的語氣與風格也變得更像自己。
concepts.solution_en: He sets fixed intake windows: view only at scheduled times, create the rest. Withdrawal is painful and he gets antsy, but when attention returns to his work, his voice and style become more distinctly his own.

concepts.dilemma: 小雅覺得要很多人認可才算值得，於是一直追大客戶與大平台。追不到就覺得自己沒價值。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoya needs mass approval to feel valid, chases major clients and platforms; failure feels worthless.
concepts.solution: 她改用「小而真」的社會證明：三封推薦信、三個詳細案例。累積很慢也不 flashy，但當她用這些去談合作，對方更信任她的可靠性，而不是只看名氣。
concepts.solution_en: She shifts to 'small and authentic' proof: three strong referrals, three detailed case studies. Accumulating is slow and unglamorous, but when negotiating, clients trust her reliability, not just fame.

concepts.dilemma: 阿哲一直覺得自己為什麼比別人更難成功，最後變成自我否定。
concepts.dilemma_en: Azhe constantly wonders why success is harder for him, slides into self-blame.
concepts.solution: 他開始把問題命名：資源、階級、語言、身份、地理。承認結構會痛，因為它不是你努力就能立刻改，但當他不再用羞恥驅動自己，就能把精力用在可行策略：找盟友、找制度縫隙、走長線。
concepts.solution_en: He names structural barriers: resources, class, language, identity, geography. Acknowledging structures hurts because effort alone can't fix them, but releasing shame lets him pursue viable strategies: find allies, find policy gaps, play long games.

concepts.dilemma: 客戶對小倩說「你如果在乎我們就會幫忙」。她一心軟就加班，最後還被嫌不夠快。
concepts.dilemma_en: A client tells Xiaoqian 'if you cared, you'd help.' She softens and overworks; they still say it's too slow.
concepts.solution: 她學會把感情和條件分開：「我在乎合作，所以我們要用清楚條款保護彼此。」說出口很難也會內疚，但當她守住一次，對方的勒索就少一次，她也越來越能把自己當人看。
concepts.solution_en: She learns to separate feeling from terms: 'I care about our work together, that's why clear terms protect us both.' Saying it is hard and guilt follows, but each time she holds the line, coercion decreases and she remembers she's human.

concepts.dilemma: 阿豪總被告知「平台規則改了你就配合」，他像永遠住在別人的地上。
concepts.dilemma_en: Aohao is told 'platforms change rules, you adapt.' He feels like he perpetually rents.
concepts.solution: 他把平台當分發不是家：作品主體放自有網站、檔案自存。這很麻煩也沒立即回報，但當平台變動時，他不再被掐住喉嚨。
concepts.solution_en: He treats platforms as distribution, not home: primary work lives on his site, files self-hosted. It's tedious with no immediate reward, but when platforms change, he controls his identity.

concepts.dilemma: 小雅把自己排成每日內容，最後每天都像交作業。她明明在發作品，心裡卻很空。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoya schedules herself as daily content; every day feels like homework. She publishes but feels hollow.
concepts.solution: 她改成「季節式」：一段時間創作、一段時間發布。切換期會焦慮、流量也會波動，但當她把創作的節奏找回來，內容反而更有生命，支持者也更願意等。
concepts.solution_en: She shifts to seasonal cycles: create phase, then publish phase. Transitions trigger anxiety and traffic fluctuates, but reclaiming creative rhythm brings content alive and supporters are more patient.

concepts.dilemma: 阿哲的工作很多是「讓事情順利」：協調、排雷、照顧團隊，但外界只看到最後那張圖。
concepts.dilemma_en: Azhe's work is mostly 'keeping things smooth': coordination, troubleshooting, caring for team, but outsiders only see the final image.
concepts.solution: 他把不可見工作寫成故事：決策、風險、取捨。整理很累也不一定有人讀，但當他在面談或提案時用這些故事說服對方，對方才知道他不是只會做圖，而是能把事做成的人。
concepts.solution_en: He narrates invisible work as stories: decisions, risks, tradeoffs. Writing is tiring and may go unread, but in interviews and pitches, these stories convince others he's not executing but orchestrating.

concepts.dilemma: 小倩在公共機構做文化案，被要求像企業一樣做KPI。她越做越覺得文化被變成報表。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoqian does cultural work at public institutions, required to hit corporate KPIs. Culture increasingly feels like spreadsheets.
concepts.solution: 她學會在KPI裡放進「質性證據」：訪談、社群回饋、案例故事。填表仍然痛苦，但當她把人留下來的改變寫進去，計畫也更容易延續，而不是只為了數字活。
concepts.solution_en: She embeds 'qualitative evidence' in KPIs: interviews, community feedback, case stories. Reporting still hurts, but documenting human change makes programs last, not just metrics.

concepts.dilemma: 有人對阿豪說「藝術家本來就該清貧」。他聽久了覺得想賺錢很羞恥。
concepts.dilemma_en: Someone tells Aohao 'artists should be poor.' After hearing it enough, earning money feels shameful.
concepts.solution: 他把錢視為「可持續的燃料」：賺錢是為了活下去、照顧自己、做更長的作品。轉念會被同溫層嘲笑，但當他活得更穩，反而更能持續創作，也更能幫助他人。
concepts.solution_en: He reframes money as 'sustainable fuel': for living, self-care, long-term work. Peers may mock the shift, but stable living enables continued creation and helping others.

concepts.dilemma: 小雅看到很多機會需要先有時間、設備、甚至先付費才能參加。她覺得自己永遠跟不上。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoya sees opportunities requiring time, equipment, or upfront fees she can't afford. She feels permanently locked out.
concepts.solution: 她改走「低成本路徑」：公開徵件、社群合作、資源共享。這條路慢、也常要自己搭平台，但當她建立起同儕網與共享資源，門檻不會消失，卻不再完全堵死她。
concepts.solution_en: She pursues a 'low-cost path': open calls, peer collaboration, resource pooling. It's slow and requires building platforms herself, but with peer networks and shared resources, barriers exist but don't block her completely.

concepts.dilemma: 阿哲只看到「成功的人」講故事，就以為照做一定行。照做不行時，他只會怪自己。
concepts.dilemma_en: Azhe hears 'success stories' and thinks following them guarantees success. When it doesn't work, he only blames himself.
concepts.solution: 他開始收集「失敗故事」與「看不見的成本」，把成功當成多因素。這會讓人有點沮喪，但也更真實；當他不再被成功故事催眠，他做選擇更穩，也更少自責。
concepts.solution_en: He gathers 'failure stories' and 'hidden costs,' treating success as multifactoral. This can feel discouraging but is more honest; undeluded by narratives, his choices steadier and he blames himself less.

concepts.dilemma: 小倩不是一天崩潰，而是每次小小倒貼、每次小小加班、每次小小吞下不合理，慢慢把自己磨光。
concepts.dilemma_en: Xiaoqian doesn't crash suddenly; she's worn down by small subsidies, small overwork, small compromises, accumulated.
concepts.solution: 她從最小的地方改：一次只修一條規則（例如先收訂金）。改的過程會反覆破功，但每守住一次，她就把自己從慢性損耗裡拉回一點，最後形成一套能保護她的工作方式。
concepts.solution_en: She changes one rule at a time (e.g., require deposits first). She'll backslide repeatedly, but each time she holds the line, she pulls herself back from slow harm, eventually building a protective system.

concepts.dilemma: 阿豪發現資源總是流向同一群人：同樣的展、同樣的獎、同樣的合作。旁邊的人再努力也像撞牆。
concepts.dilemma_en: Aohao notices resources flow to the same people: same exhibitions, same awards, same collaborations. Others hit walls despite effort.
concepts.solution: 他加入一個小型資源共享行動：公開機會、把部分收入拿來支持新手、共用設備。這會引來質疑「你在做慈善嗎」，也會遇到不公平的挫折，但當社群開始長出新的機會循環，他也不再只是等待被選中，而是在一起改變分配方式。
concepts.solution_en: He joins a resource-sharing initiative: publicize opportunities, share some income supporting newcomers, pool equipment. Critics ask 'is this charity?' and setbacks come, but when new opportunity cycles emerge in community, selection stops being passive; redistribution becomes collective reimagining.
